signals.title: GOP Rep. Tom Kean says depression diagnosis caused months-long absence from Congress

signals.title: California delays emissions reporting deadline by 3 months
signals.summary: The California Air Resources Board said it will delay the compliance deadline for California’s SB 253 — the Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act — and propose “limited changes.”

signals.title: Duke gives up offshore wind lease for ‘partial’ reimbursement under Interior deal
signals.summary: A Duke Energy subsidiary paid $155 million for the lease in 2022, saying it could support up to 1.6 GW of capacity. The utility now says it will “refocus” nearly $129 million to projects that may include new nuclear, natural gas and grid enhancements.

signals.title: From wildfire risk to resilience: How asset investment tools can mitigate the growing challenge
signals.summary: A shift in asset investment planning can help utilities quantify risk and defend wildfire mitigation plans to regulators, stakeholders and the public, writes Jeff Pauska at IFS Copperleaf.

signals.title: FDA lets Philip Morris market Zyn nicotine pouches as less harmful than cigarettes
signals.summary: The Trump administration has expanded access to nicotine products despite concerns from public health critics about youth use and addiction.

signals.summary: The Council of Economic Advisers has provided the president with information and advice on both domestic and international economic policy since 1946.

signals.title: Trump's massive defense budget, depleted war machine, spark U.S. state battle for business and jobs
signals.summary: Trump's huge defense budget request and a race to replenish weapons stocks while building hypersonic missiles lead to war between U.S. states for jobs.

signals.summary: President Donald Trump attended oral arguments in the case, underscoring his staunch opposition to granting automatic citizenship to many immigrants' babies.

signals.title: Japan spent $74 billion propping up the yen. Investors say the real battle is with the Fed
